scope: balance/monitor/drift/saturation/alarm cohort expansion audit and shared strict-v3 blocker refresh
learner_facing_quality_improvement: This wave improves learner-facing RAG maintenance by showing that the balance-monitor cohort has grown and remains retrieval-relevant, while the real requeue risk now comes from six strict-v3 regressions outside the cohort; that separation prevents wasted edits on safe learner docs.

verification.frontmatter_scan_command: python - <<'PY' ... filename contains balance|rebalance|monitor|drift|saturation|alarm, excluding Pilot lock ... PY
verification.lint_probe_result: all seven frontmatter-present docs in the cohort still fail the current H1-first authoring lint, so local admission is still blocked even before shared strict-v3 validation is considered
verification.global_gate_result: the live corpus now fails strict-v3 with 26 violations across six non-cohort docs: three software-engineering docs with unsupported `intent` values and three spring docs with missing `expected_queries` plus invalid `linked_paths`
verification.retrieval_probe_result: eval and planner artifacts still reference `anti-corruption-mapping-drift`, `http2-max-concurrent-streams-pending-queue-saturation`, `permission-model-drift-authz-graph-design`, `semaphore-monitor-basics`, and `load-average-triage-cpu-saturation-cgroup-throttling-io-wait`, confirming active learner-facing demand before full v3 admission is restored

priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is already retrieval-visible and strengthens an underrepresented software-engineering path once shared gate debt is cleared.

guardrails: No Pilot-locked corpus file was modified.
